hub_subtitle: Inglês para a vida real — falar, entender e ter autonomia no idioma

lesson.menu_desc: Describing the two creatures who run your house -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 4 -- My Dogs
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 4 -- My Dogs

listenings.text: She was the most frightened animal I have ever seen. Skinny, filthy, hiding under a parked car, and she would not come out for anything. It took me three days and a lot of cheap sausages. Now, four years later, she is a big, lazy, ridiculous creature who sleeps on my sofa and complains if the food is late. People say rescue dogs are grateful. I do not know about grateful. I think she just decided that this was hers now, and honestly, she was right. The house is hers. I only pay for it.

The morning walk is not relaxing, whatever people tell you. One of them is old and slow and wants to smell every single thing, and the other one is young and completely exhausting. So I am being pulled in two directions for forty minutes. But there is a moment, usually around the corner by the field, when they both stop at the same time and just look at something. A bird, or nothing at all. And it is very quiet, and I am not thinking about anything. That is the moment I go out for.

extra_audio.text: First, Tom asks Ana what her dogs are like. Then Ana describes Mel, the older one, as calm and lazy. Next, she says the younger one is noisy and exhausting. After that, she explains how Mel was frightened when she found her. Finally, she admits that the dogs decided the house was theirs.
